sections.title: Placeholder proof slots for clients, partners, press, or community signals.
{{BRAND}} starts with a clean Mantle content shape
sections.body: Use this block for the short positioning paragraph every useful presence site needs.

items.body: {{BRAND}} can explain the offer, audience, and next step here without committing to a final visual direction.
The copy is intentionally plain so the owner can replace it with real voice, proof, and details after launch.

sections.title: What this Mantle starter is ready to hold
sections.body: Each item is a placeholder for a common presence-site section, not a fixed theme.

items.title: Positioning
items.body: A concise statement of who this is for and what the site should make obvious.

items.title: Offer
items.body: A place for services, packages, products, or the first useful thing to understand.

items.title: Contact path
items.body: A simple route for serious inquiries before richer workflows are added.

items.title: Proof
items.body: Slots for outcomes, client names, quotes, partners, or lightweight case notes.

items.title: Trust
items.body: Short answers to visitor doubts before they decide to reach out.

items.title: Editable structure
items.body: A section model that is easy for the owner or team to rewrite.

sections.title: A compact set of starter blocks, not a finished brand
sections.body: Use this area to decide which pieces stay, which become real content, and which should disappear.

items.title: Main story
items.body: The hero and about blocks establish the first plain-English narrative.

items.title: Offer snapshot
items.body: Feature cards can become services, capabilities, product lines, or use cases.

items.title: Proof snapshot
items.body: Proof blocks give Mantle something concrete to refine instead of an empty page.

sections.title: Small signals show where real numbers can land
sections.body: These are placeholder counters for reach, work, speed, outcomes, or other honest proof.

sections.title: Quote placeholders with a real replacement shape
sections.body: Keep the structure, then swap in honest quotes, partner blurbs, or short outcome notes.

items.quote: This placeholder quote should become a concrete sentence from a real client, partner, or user.
items.name: Mia Chen

items.quote: Use this card for a short proof point that makes the offer easier to trust.
items.name: Alex Rivera

items.quote: Mantle keeps the first draft plain so the site can become specific quickly.
items.name: Sam Patel

sections.title: Starter questions to replace with real visitor concerns
sections.body: Use this block for clear answers before adding heavier support or content flows.

items.title: What should this site explain first?
items.body: {{BRAND}} should answer the visitor's first practical question before asking them to take action.

items.title: What should the first call to action be?
items.body: Keep one obvious next step: contact, book, subscribe, join, or read more.

items.title: Where does proof belong?
items.body: Use logos, metrics, quotes, or work examples only when they help visitors decide.

items.title: Can this grow after launch?
items.body: Yes. Start with the presence page, then add richer Mantle features only when the site needs them.

sections.title: Give the placeholder site one useful action
sections.body: This section can become an email card, booking path, intake form, or simple contact surface.
sections.footerBody: Ask only for the details needed to reply well; add richer intake later.

items.title: Email
items.body: Swap in the inbox that should receive first inquiries.

items.title: Response
items.body: Set a realistic response expectation for visitors.

items.title: Location
items.body: Keep this broad for remote-first or service-area businesses.

sections.title: A minimal form for the first serious message
sections.body: Use these fields as a placeholder intake shape before adding validation, routing, or richer workflows.
sections.footerBody: The first version stores the message. Connect email delivery when the owner is ready.

items.body: Only ask for the essentials
Set a simple response promise
Add real routing details later

sections.title: Turn this Mantle placeholder into {{BRAND}}
sections.body: Keep the useful blocks, delete the rest, and replace every placeholder with real words.
